essence: The image captures the weight of a moment that exists between memory and dream — an ordinary domestic scene rendered with such emotional precision that it transcends into the sacred. We are intruders witnessing something private: a figure caught in the threshold between staying and leaving, between presence and absence. The light behaves not according to physics but according to feeling — pooling where attention should go, casting shadows that seem to listen. One worn object anchors the ethereal to the real: a coffee ring, a creased photograph, a missing button. This is the texture of déjà vu made visible — the familiar made strange by perfect stillness, a painting you remember seeing but never actually saw.

facets.name: Emotional Intrusion
facets.description: The viewer feels they have walked into a room they shouldn't be in — witnessing something private, sacred, tender

facets.name: Dream-Logic Light
facets.description: Illumination that obeys emotion rather than physics — light that reveals character, shadows that feel sentient

facets.name: Artifact of the Real
facets.description: One worn, lived-in object that grounds the ethereal — the imperfection that proves life

facets.name: Temporal Suspension
facets.description: The moment between moments — not the action but the breath before, the threshold between states

facets.name: Material Specificity
facets.description: The medium participates in meaning — cracked varnish, canvas tooth, photogravure grain, visible brushwork

selected_emotions: Anemoia (nostalgia for a time never lived)
